mapping.a_element: osp(5|2) superalgebra with its symmetry-breaking subalgebra chain
mapping.b_element: the Galois group acting on the covering of linear categories

mapping.a_element: branching of the 64-dimensional irreducible representation into subalgebra multiplets
mapping.b_element: the spectral-sequence filtration decomposing the Hochschild-Mitchell (co)homology

brief_justification: The only overlap is generic — both invoke an algebraic symmetry structure and some notion of 'decomposition'. The actual machinery differs: paper A uses representation-theoretic branching rules of Lie superalgebras under a symmetry-breaking subalgebra chain to match multiplet degeneracies, whereas paper B uses a homological Cartan-Leray spectral sequence to compute Hochschild-Mitchell cohomology of a Galois covering. A branching rule is not a spectral sequence, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving process holds.
